response:
Here is a complete, pytest-compliant test suite covering all requested cases, following modern pytest best practices:
```python
import pytest
# UPDATE THIS IMPORT TO MATCH YOUR ACTUAL MODULE
from your_code_module import matrix_transpose


class TestMatrixTranspose:
    """Full test suite for matrix_transpose function"""

    @pytest.mark.parametrize(
        "input_matrix, expected_output",
        [
            # ------------------------------
            # EDGE CASES
            # ------------------------------
            ([], []),  # Completely empty input matrix
            ([[7]], [[7]]),  # 1x1 single element matrix
            ([[]], []),  # Matrix with only an empty row
            # ------------------------------
            # HAPPY PATH STANDARD CASES
            # ------------------------------
            # 2x2 Square matrix
            ([[1, 2], [3, 4]], [[1, 3], [2, 4]]),
            # 3x3 Square matrix
            ([[1, 2, 3], [4, 5, 6], [7, 8, 9]], [[1, 4, 7], [2, 5, 8], [3, 6, 9]]),
            # Rectangular: tall matrix (more rows than columns)
            ([[1, 2], [3, 4], [5, 6]], [[1, 3, 5], [2, 4, 6]]),
            # Rectangular: wide matrix (more columns than rows)
            ([[10, 20, 30, 40], [50, 60, 70, 80]], [[10, 50], [20, 60], [30, 70], [40, 80]]),
            # Single row vector
            ([[9, 8, 7, 6]], [[9], [8], [7], [6]]),
            # Single column vector
            ([[9], [8], [7], [6]], [[9, 8, 7, 6]]),
            # ------------------------------
            # TYPE SAFETY CASES
            # ------------------------------
            # Matrix with mixed data types (function works for any value)
            ([[True, "hello"], [None, 3.1415]], [[True, None], ["hello", 3.1415]]),
        ],
        ids=[
            "empty_matrix",
            "1x1_single_element",
            "empty_single_row_matrix",
            "2x2_square_matrix",
            "3x3_square_matrix",
            "tall_rectangular_matrix",
            "wide_rectangular_matrix",
            "single_row_vector",
            "single_column_vector",
            "mixed_data_types"
        ]
    )
    def test_valid_inputs_produce_correct_transpose(self, input_matrix, expected_output):
        # Preserve copy to verify original input is NOT mutated
        original_input_backup = [row.copy() for row in input_matrix]

        result = matrix_transpose(input_matrix)

        assert result == expected_output
        # Verify pure function behaviour: original input remains unchanged
        assert input_matrix == original_input_backup

    # ------------------------------
    # ERROR CASES
    # ------------------------------
    @pytest.mark.parametrize(
        "invalid_input",
        [
            None,
            42,
            "string_instead_of_matrix",
            object(),
            [1, 2, 3, 4],  # Flat list (not list of rows)
        ]
    )
    def test_non_matrix_inputs_raise_appropriate_errors(self, invalid_input):
        with pytest.raises((TypeError, IndexError)):
            matrix_transpose(invalid_input)

    def test_jagged_uneven_rows_raise_index_error(self):
        """This transpose implementation expects uniform row lengths"""
        jagged_matrix = [[1, 2, 3], [4, 5], [6, 7, 8, 9]]
        with pytest.raises(IndexError):
            matrix_transpose(jagged_matrix)

    def test_mixed_empty_and_full_rows_raise_error(self):
        malformed_matrix = [[1, 2], [], [3, 4]]
        with pytest.raises(IndexError):
            matrix_transpose(malformed_matrix)
```

---

### Test Coverage Breakdown:
| Category | Coverage Details |
|---|---|
| ✅ Happy Path | All standard matrix shapes: square, rectangular, row vectors, column vectors |
| ✅ Edge Cases | Empty input, 1x1 matrix, empty rows, mixed data types |
| ✅ Error Cases | Non-matrix inputs, jagged/uneven row matrices, malformed input structures |
| ✅ Extra Validation | Verifies the original input matrix is **never mutated** (pure function behaviour) |

This suite will correctly validate both the intended behaviour *and* the known limitations of the provided matrix transpose implementation.
